purpose: The dashboard sidebar — primary navigation surface. Renders four top tab buttons (Code Explorer, Dashboards, Git Manager, Preview), an add-dashboard button (opens folder picker → createDashboard → saveDashboardProject), and a list of dashboards with status dots, custom names (with double-click rename), drag-and-drop reorder (within IDE-vs-non-IDE sections only), per-item Agent Chat + Delete actions, unread-chat badges, and a queue summary at the bottom. Owns local state for collapse, rename, drag (dragId/dragOverId/dragCounterRef), and delete-confirmation popup. IDE dashboards are filtered to only those whose workspace still exists in `ideWorkspaces`; orphaned mappings trigger automatic cleanup via removeWorkspaceDashboard + deleteDashboard. Settings button at the footer opens the SettingsModal.
gotchas: Drag-and-drop reorder only works within the same SECTION (IDE↔IDE or non-IDE↔non-IDE) — the handler explicitly bails when sourceIsIde !== targetIsIde, but there's no visual cue showing the rejection.
Reorder dispatches REORDER_DASHBOARDS on the FULL allDashboards order (not the filtered view) so dropping does not accidentally trigger orphan cleanup.
Orphaned IDE dashboard cleanup runs on every ideWorkspaces/dashboardList change — if dashboardList loads after ideWorkspaces, no cleanup yet (early return when empty list).

Delete confirmation popup branches into THREE flows: agent-active (with hasTaskData sub-branch), no-agent-but-has-data, and immediate-delete. The 'don't allow deleting the last dashboard' guard only fires in the immediate-delete branch.
Custom names take precedence over project names in getDisplayName, but IDE dashboards always show 'IDE - {project}' and ignore custom names — this surprises users who try to rename an IDE entry.
--sidebar-width CSS variable is mutated in an effect (52px collapsed / 220px expanded) — components using it should treat it as dynamic, not static.
Drag handlers set e.target.style.opacity directly via DOM mutation; if React re-renders mid-drag, the inline style is preserved on the stale node.
patterns: useMemo over ideWorkspaces builds two parallel structures (Set + Map) to avoid scanning the array twice during sort.
Dashboard render keys off id; itemClasses array is filtered+joined to avoid stray spaces.
All async electronAPI calls swallow errors with .catch(()=>{}) at the call site — failures are silent UX-wise.
drag enter/leave uses a counter (dragCounterRef) to handle nested elements correctly — without this, hovering over a child of the drop target fires extra leave events.
conventions: Sidebar buttons use `sidebar-tab` + `.active` modifier; tab rows are .sidebar-tab-row inside .sidebar-tab-bar.
Per-item action buttons class chain: dashboard-item-action-btn (+chat-action-btn / dashboard-delete-btn).
Inline rename input commits on Enter or blur, cancels on Escape; pressing Enter prevents default form submission.
